full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Checking how to pass a precomputed kernel to sklearn SVC
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: how do i implement K_DL from the brunner 2012 paper in python using sklearn? the formula is k(a,c) + k(b,d) - k(a,d) - k(b,c)
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: I looked up sklearn docs myself to verify. The kernel='precomputed' usage and passing K_train/K_test matrices is my own implementation.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understanding gamma for RBF kernel on PCA features
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: my svm is giving weird accuracy like 100% on train but 50% on test, is this overfitting or smthng wrong with my kernel matrix?
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Used as a starting point estimate. I manually tuned gamma=0.01 based on observing cross-validated accuracy.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Can you write out the K_DL formula from Brunner 2012 so I can double check my implementation?
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: I used this only to sanity-check my reading of Equation (6). The code I wrote is entirely mine.
